Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First Ill re read the normative process to make sure I understand the rules correctly The process starts with Receive order Then the next step must be Check inventory After that depending on whether inventory is sufficient or not the path diverges

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If inventory is sufficient Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order If inventory is insufficient Cancel order Close order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key constraints Authorize payment must come after Check inventory and before packaging Package items must be after authorization and before shipping Ship order cant happen after cancellation Close order is always the last step

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Activities in order Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order This looks correct The order is received checked authorized packaged shipped then closed All steps follow the expected flow So no violation here

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Activities Receive order Check inventory Package items Authorize payment Ship order Close order Wait here after checking inventory they package items before authorizing payment But according to the rules authorization should come before packaging So this is a violation Package items should only occur after authorization So Case 2 is violating the process

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Activities Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order Hmm after authorizing payment the order is canceled But cancellation should happen if inventory is insufficient However the problem is that after cancellation they still ship the order The rule says that once canceled you shouldnt proceed to shipping Also the sequence here is check inventory authorize payment then cancel But if inventory was insufficient the process should go to cancel directly without authorizing payment So two issues here authorizing payment before knowing inventory is insufficient and then shipping after cancellation So Case 3 is definitely violating

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Activities Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order This seems correct Inventory check shows insufficient so cancel then close No issues here So no violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Activities Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Ship order Close order Wait here after authorization they ship without packaging The rule says package items must come before shipping So this is a violation They skipped packaging So Case 5 is violating

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the violating cases are 2 3 and 5

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me double check Case 2 Package before authorization Yes thats a violation Case 3 Cancel after authorization then ship Both the order of cancel after authorization if inventory was insufficient and shipping after cancel are violations Case 5 Missing packaging step So shipping without packaging Yes thats correct The other cases 1 and 4 are okay
